Schema:
organizations(id, name, industry, region, created_at, status)
users(id, email, full_name, role, created_at)
organization_users(organization_id, user_id, title, is_admin)
plans(id, name, billing_interval, monthly_price_usd)
subscriptions(id, organization_id, plan_id, started_at, ended_at, status, seats)
invoices(id, organization_id, subscription_id, invoice_date, due_date, paid_at, status, total_amount_usd)
invoice_line_items(id, invoice_id, description, quantity, unit_price_usd, amount_usd)
usage_events(id, organization_id, event_name, event_timestamp, billable_units)
support_tickets(id, organization_id, opened_at, resolved_at, priority, status, category)

Business rules:
Revenue is invoices.total_amount_usd or invoice_line_items.amount_usd. Active organizations have status = 'active'. Only read-only SQL is allowed. Queries should return deterministic results.

Question:
Which organizations generated the highest total paid invoice revenue?
SELECT o.name, SUM(i.total_amount_usd) AS revenue FROM organizations o JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id WHERE i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.name ORDER BY revenue DESC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Schema:
customers(id, full_name, segment, region, created_at)
sellers(id, seller_name, country, tier, joined_at)
categories(id, category_name, department)
products(id, seller_id, category_id, product_name, unit_price_usd, status)
orders(id, customer_id, ordered_at, status, channel)
order_items(id, order_id, product_id, quantity, item_amount_usd)
payments(id, order_id, payment_method, paid_at, payment_status, amount_usd)
shipments(id, order_id, carrier, shipped_at, delivered_at, shipment_status)
returns(id, order_item_id, requested_at, return_status, refund_amount_usd)

Business rules:
GMV is the sum of order_items.item_amount_usd. Paid orders have payments.payment_status = 'paid'. Returned units are identified by returns linked to order_items.

Question:
Which sellers generated the highest paid GMV?

SELECT s.seller_name, SUM(oi.item_amount_usd) AS gmv_usd FROM sellers s JOIN products p ON p.seller_id = s.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN payments pay ON pay.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE pay.payment_status = 'paid' GROUP BY s.seller_name ORDER BY gmv_usd DESC, s.seller_name ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Schema:
borrowers(id, full_name, state, risk_tier, created_at)
applications(id, borrower_id, submitted_at, product_type, requested_amount_usd, application_status)
loans(id, borrower_id, application_id, originated_at, principal_usd, interest_rate_pct, loan_status)
repayments(id, loan_id, due_date, paid_at, repayment_status, amount_usd)
cards(id, borrower_id, issued_at, card_status, credit_limit_usd)
card_transactions(id, card_id, merchant_name, transacted_at, category, amount_usd, transaction_status)

Business rules:
Active loans have loan_status = 'active'. Missed repayments have repayment_status = 'missed'. Approved applications are applications.application_status = 'approved'.

Question:
What is active loan principal by borrower risk tier?

SELECT b.risk_tier, SUM(l.principal_usd) AS active_principal_usd FROM borrowers b JOIN loans l ON l.borrower_id = b.id WHERE l.loan_status = 'active' GROUP BY b.risk_tier ORDER BY active_principal_usd DESC, b.risk_tier ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Schema:
departments(id, department_name, region)
employees(id, department_id, full_name, title, hired_at, employment_status)
job_openings(id, department_id, opening_title, opened_at, opening_status, target_salary_usd)
candidates(id, full_name, source_channel, current_city)
applications(id, candidate_id, job_opening_id, applied_at, application_status)
interviews(id, application_id, interview_stage, interviewer_employee_id, scheduled_at, interview_result)
offers(id, application_id, offered_at, offer_status, annual_salary_usd)

Business rules:
Hires are applications with application_status = 'hired'. Open openings use opening_status = 'open'.

Question:
How many open job openings does each department currently have?

SELECT d.department_name, COUNT(j.id) AS open_openings FROM departments d LEFT JOIN job_openings j ON j.department_id = d.id AND j.opening_status = 'open' GROUP BY d.department_name ORDER BY open_openings DESC, d.department_name ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Schema:
warehouses(id, warehouse_name, city, region)
carriers(id, carrier_name, fleet_type, home_region)
routes(id, origin_warehouse_id, destination_city, route_distance_km, route_status)

packages(id, shipment_id, package_weight_kg, service_level, package_status)
delivery_events(id, shipment_id, event_timestamp, event_type, event_city)
fuel_logs(id, carrier_id, route_id, logged_at, liters_used, fuel_cost_usd)

Business rules:
Delivered shipments use shipment_status = 'delivered'. Active routes use route_status = 'active'.

Question:
What is the delivered package count by carrier?

SELECT c.carrier_name, COUNT(p.id) AS delivered_package_count FROM carriers c JOIN shipments s ON s.carrier_id = c.id JOIN packages p ON p.shipment_id = s.id WHERE s.shipment_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY c.carrier_name ORDER BY delivered_package_count DESC, c.carrier_name ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Schema:
patients(id, full_name, birth_year, payer_type, home_city)
clinics(id, clinic_name, city, specialty_focus)
providers(id, clinic_id, provider_name, specialty, provider_status)

prescriptions(id, appointment_id, drug_name, days_supply, prescription_status)
lab_orders(id, appointment_id, test_name, ordered_at, lab_status)

Business rules:
Completed visits use appointment_status = 'completed'. Paid claims use claim_status = 'paid'.

Question:
How much paid claim revenue did each clinic collect?

SELECT c.clinic_name, SUM(cl.paid_amount_usd) AS paid_revenue_usd FROM clinics c JOIN providers p ON p.clinic_id = c.id JOIN appointments a ON a.provider_id = p.id JOIN claims cl ON cl.appointment_id = a.id WHERE cl.claim_status = 'paid' GROUP BY c.clinic_name ORDER BY paid_revenue_usd DESC, c.clinic_name ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Schema:
organizations(id, org_name, org_type, region)

assessment_attempts(id, assessment_id, learner_id, attempted_at, score, attempt_status)
subscriptions(id, organization_id, started_at, plan_name, monthly_amount_usd, subscription_status)

Business rules:
Active enrollments use enrollment_status = 'active'. Passed attempts use attempt_status = 'passed'.

Question:
How many active enrollments does each course have?

SELECT c.course_title, COUNT(e.id) AS active_enrollments FROM courses c LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON e.course_id = c.id AND e.enrollment_status = 'active' GROUP BY c.course_title ORDER BY active_enrollments DESC, c.course_title ASC

Schema:
accounts(id, account_name, segment, region)
campaigns(id, account_id, campaign_name, channel, campaign_status)
ad_groups(id, campaign_id, ad_group_name, audience_type)
ads(id, ad_group_id, creative_name, ad_status)

lead_events(id, lead_id, event_timestamp, event_type)
conversions(id, lead_id, converted_at, conversion_value_usd, conversion_status)

Business rules:
Qualified leads use lead_status = 'qualified'. Won conversions use conversion_status = 'won'.

Question:
Which campaigns generated the highest won conversion value?

SELECT c.campaign_name, SUM(conv.conversion_value_usd) AS won_value_usd FROM campaigns c JOIN leads l ON l.campaign_id = c.id JOIN conversions conv ON conv.lead_id = l.id WHERE conv.conversion_status = 'won' GROUP BY c.campaign_name ORDER BY won_value_usd DESC, c.campaign_name ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Schema:
properties(id, property_name, city, brand_tier)
rooms(id, property_id, room_number, room_type, room_status)
guests(id, full_name, loyalty_tier, home_country)
reservations(id, guest_id, room_id, check_in_date, check_out_date, reservation_status, nightly_rate_usd)

staff(id, property_id, staff_name, department)
housekeeping_tasks(id, room_id, assigned_staff_id, task_date, task_status)

Business rules:
Checked-out reservations use reservation_status = 'checked_out'. Closed folios use folio_status = 'closed'.

Question:
What closed folio revenue did each property generate?

SELECT p.property_name, SUM(f.total_amount_usd) AS closed_folio_revenue_usd FROM properties p JOIN rooms r ON r.property_id = p.id JOIN reservations res ON res.room_id = r.id JOIN folios f ON f.reservation_id = res.id WHERE f.folio_status = 'closed' GROUP BY p.property_name ORDER BY closed_folio_revenue_usd DESC, p.property_name ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

organizations(id, org_name, business_unit, region)

vulnerabilities(id, asset_id, vuln_key, severity, discovered_at, vuln_status)
patch_jobs(id, asset_id, scheduled_at, completed_at, patch_status)

Business rules:
Open incidents use incident_status = 'open'. Remediated vulnerabilities use vuln_status = 'remediated'. Failed logins use auth_result = 'failure'.

Question:
How many open alerts does each asset currently have?
SELECT a.asset_name, COUNT(al.id) AS open_alerts FROM assets a LEFT JOIN alerts al ON al.asset_id = a.id AND al.alert_status = 'open' GROUP BY a.asset_name ORDER BY open_alerts DESC, a.asset_name ASC

machine_downtime(id, plant_id, machine_name, outage_started_at, outage_hours, outage_reason)

Business rules:
Released work orders use work_order_status = 'released'. Received shipments use shipment_status = 'received'. Failed quality checks use quality_status = 'failed'.

Question:
What is total ordered component cost by supplier?

SELECT s.supplier_name, SUM(pi.ordered_qty * pi.unit_cost_usd) AS ordered_cost_usd FROM suppliers s JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id = s.id JOIN po_items pi ON pi.purchase_order_id = po.id GROUP BY s.supplier_name ORDER BY ordered_cost_usd DESC, s.supplier_name ASC

Question:
Which agents have the highest closed deal volume?

SELECT a.agent_name, SUM(c.closed_amount_usd) AS closed_volume_usd FROM agents a JOIN listings l ON l.listing_agent_id = a.id JOIN offers o ON o.listing_id = l.id JOIN closings c ON c.offer_id = o.id WHERE c.closing_status = 'closed' GROUP BY a.agent_name ORDER BY closed_volume_usd DESC, a.agent_name ASC

Question:
What is the total number of seats by region for active organizations?

  o.region,
  SUM(s.seats) AS total_seats
FROM organizations AS o
JOIN subscriptions AS s
  ON s.organization_id = o.id
WHERE o.status = 'active'
GROUP BY o.region
ORDER BY o.region;

Question:
Which active organizations had paid revenue in January 2025, and how much did each organization collect?

SELECT o.name AS organization_name, ROUND(SUM(i.total_amount_usd), 2) AS paid_revenue_usd
FROM organizations o
JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id
WHERE o.status = 'active'
  AND i.status = 'paid'
  AND i.paid_at >= '2025-01-01'
  AND i.paid_at < '2025-02-01'
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY paid_revenue_usd DESC, o.name ASC;
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Question:

Question:
What is the revenue per seat for each active organization with an active subscription?

WITH active_subscriptions AS ( SELECT organization_id, SUM(seats) AS total_seats FROM subscriptions WHERE status = 'active' GROUP BY organization_id ), revenue_by_org AS ( SELECT i.organization_id, SUM(i.total_amount_usd) AS total_revenue_usd FROM invoices i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.id = i.subscription_id JOIN organizations o ON o.id = i.organization_id WHERE o.status = 'active' AND s.status = 'active' AND i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY i.organization_id ) SELECT o.name AS organization_name, ROUND(COALESCE(revenue_by_org.total_revenue_usd, 0) / NULLIF(active_subscriptions.total_seats, 0), 2) AS revenue_per_seat_usd FROM organizations o JOIN active_subscriptions ON active_subscriptions.organization_id = o.id LEFT JOIN revenue_by_org ON revenue_by_org.organization_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'active' ORDER BY revenue_per_seat_usd DESC, organization_name ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Schema:
organizations(id, name, industry, region, created_at, status)
users(id, email, full_name, role, created_at)
organization_users(organization_id, user_id, title, is_admin)
plans(id, name, billing_interval, monthly_price_usd)
subscriptions(id, organization_id, plan_id, started_at, ended_at, status, seats)
invoices(id, organization_id, subscription_id, invoice_date, due_date, paid_at, status, total_amount_usd)
invoice_line_items(id, invoice_id, description, quantity, unit_price_usd, amount_usd)
usage_events(id, organization_id, event_name, event_timestamp, billable_units)
support_tickets(id, organization_id, opened_at, resolved_at, priority, status, category)

Business rules:
Revenue is invoices.total_amount_usd or invoice_line_items.amount_usd. Active organizations have status = 'active'. Only read-only SQL is allowed. Queries should return deterministic results.

Question:
What is the total premium support revenue by organization?

SELECT
  o.name AS organization_name,
  COALESCE(SUM(li.amount_usd), 0) AS total_premium_support_revenue_usd
FROM organizations AS o
JOIN invoices AS i
  ON i.organization_id = o.id
JOIN invoice_line_items AS li
  ON li.invoice_id = i.id
WHERE LOWER(li.description) LIKE '%premium support%'
GROUP BY o.id, o.name
ORDER BY total_premium_support_revenue_usd DESC, o.name ASC;
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Question:
For each organization, what is the average number of days it takes to pay invoices that were paid?

SELECT o.id AS organization_id, o.name AS organization_name, ROUND(AVG(julianday(i.paid_at) - julianday(i.invoice_date)), 2) AS avg_days_to_pay_paid_invoices FROM invoices AS i JOIN organizations AS o ON o.id = i.organization_id WHERE i.status = 'paid' AND i.paid_at IS NOT NULL AND i.invoice_date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY o.id, o.name ORDER BY avg_days_to_pay_paid_invoices DESC, o.name ASC;

Question:
Which organizations have total line-item revenue exactly matching their invoice totals?

WITH line_item_totals AS (
  SELECT i.organization_id, SUM(ili.amount_usd) AS line_item_revenue
  FROM invoices i
  JOIN invoice_line_items ili ON ili.invoice_id = i.id
  GROUP BY i.organization_id
), invoice_totals AS (
  SELECT organization_id, SUM(total_amount_usd) AS invoice_revenue
  FROM invoices
  GROUP BY organization_id
)
SELECT o.name
FROM organizations o
JOIN line_item_totals lit ON lit.organization_id = o.id
JOIN invoice_totals it ON it.organization_id = o.id
WHERE ROUND(lit.line_item_revenue, 2) = ROUND(it.invoice_revenue, 2)
ORDER BY o.name

Question:
Which departments generated the highest paid GMV, and how do they rank from highest to lowest?

SELECT c.department, SUM(oi.item_amount_usd) AS paid_gmv, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(oi.item_amount_usd) DESC, c.department ASC) AS gmv_rank FROM orders o JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT order_id FROM payments WHERE payment_status = "paid") p ON p.order_id = o.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products pr ON pr.id = oi.product_id JOIN categories c ON c.id = pr.category_id GROUP BY c.department ORDER BY paid_gmv DESC, c.department ASC;
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Question:
Which products generated the most paid GMV across paid orders? Return the top 5 products with their seller names and rank them from highest to lowest paid GMV.

WITH paid_orders AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT order_id
  FROM payments
  WHERE payment_status = 'paid'
), ranked_products AS (
  SELECT
    p.id AS product_id,
    p.product_name,
    s.seller_name,
    SUM(oi.item_amount_usd) AS paid_gmv_usd,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      ORDER BY SUM(oi.item_amount_usd) DESC, p.product_name ASC, p.id ASC
    ) AS product_rank
  FROM paid_orders po
  JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = po.order_id
  JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id
  JOIN sellers s ON s.id = p.seller_id
  GROUP BY p.id, p.product_name, s.seller_name
)
SELECT product_rank, product_name, seller_name, paid_gmv_usd
FROM ranked_products
WHERE product_rank <= 5
ORDER BY product_rank;
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Question:
Which 5 categories generated the highest paid GMV, and what rank did each category have when categories are ordered by paid GMV descending, then category name ascending?

WITH category_paid_gmv AS ( SELECT c.id AS category_id, c.category_name, SUM(oi.item_amount_usd) AS paid_gmv FROM categories c JOIN products p ON p.category_id = c.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN payments pay ON pay.order_id = o.id WHERE pay.payment_status = 'paid' GROUP BY c.id, c.category_name ), ranked_categories AS ( SELECT category_name, paid_gmv, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY paid_gmv DESC, category_name ASC) AS category_rank FROM category_paid_gmv ) SELECT category_name, paid_gmv, category_rank FROM ranked_categories WHERE category_rank <= 5 ORDER BY category_rank;

SELECT ao.owner_team, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS alerted_asset_count FROM asset_owners ao JOIN assets a ON a.id = ao.asset_id JOIN alerts al ON al.asset_id = a.id GROUP BY ao.owner_team HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) > 0 ORDER BY alerted_asset_count DESC, ao.owner_team ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Schema:
organizations(id, org_name, business_unit, region)
assets(id, organization_id, asset_name, asset_type, criticality, asset_status)
asset_owners(id, asset_id, owner_name, owner_team)
detections(id, detection_name, severity, tactic)
alerts(id, asset_id, detection_id, opened_at, alert_status)
incidents(id, organization_id, incident_name, severity, incident_status, opened_at)
incident_alerts(id, incident_id, alert_id)
auth_events(id, asset_id, event_timestamp, actor_email, auth_result, source_ip)
vulnerabilities(id, asset_id, vuln_key, severity, discovered_at, vuln_status)
patch_jobs(id, asset_id, scheduled_at, completed_at, patch_status)

Business rules:
Open incidents use incident_status = 'open'. Remediated vulnerabilities use vuln_status = 'remediated'. Failed logins use auth_result = 'failure'.

Question:

Question:
How many paid orders used each payment method in each customer region?

Question:
Which sellers have an average sold item amount above 200 USD?

SELECT s.seller_name, AVG(oi.item_amount_usd) AS avg_item_amount_usd FROM sellers s JOIN products pr ON pr.seller_id = s.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = pr.id GROUP BY s.seller_name HAVING AVG(oi.item_amount_usd) > 200 ORDER BY avg_item_amount_usd DESC, s.seller_name ASC

Question:
For each customer, what is net paid GMV after subtracting refunds?

SELECT c.full_name, SUM(oi.item_amount_usd) - COALESCE(SUM(r.refund_amount_usd), 0) AS net_paid_gmv_usd FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id LEFT JOIN returns r ON r.order_item_id = oi.id WHERE p.payment_status = 'paid' GROUP BY c.full_name ORDER BY net_paid_gmv_usd DESC, c.full_name ASC

Question:
Which categories were purchased by both customer segments?

SELECT cat.category_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.segment) AS distinct_segments FROM categories cat JOIN products pr ON pr.category_id = cat.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = pr.id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN customers c ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY cat.category_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.segment) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT segment) FROM customers) ORDER BY cat.category_name ASC

SELECT res.id, res.check_in_date, res.check_out_date, r.room_number, p.property_name FROM reservations res JOIN rooms r ON r.id = res.room_id JOIN properties p ON p.id = r.property_id ORDER BY res.check_in_date ASC, res.id ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.

Schema:
properties(id, property_name, city, brand_tier)
rooms(id, property_id, room_number, room_type, room_status)
guests(id, full_name, loyalty_tier, home_country)
reservations(id, guest_id, room_id, check_in_date, check_out_date, reservation_status, nightly_rate_usd)
folios(id, reservation_id, folio_status, total_amount_usd)
folio_items(id, folio_id, item_type, item_amount_usd)
staff(id, property_id, staff_name, department)
housekeeping_tasks(id, room_id, assigned_staff_id, task_date, task_status)

Business rules:
Checked-out reservations use reservation_status = 'checked_out'. Closed folios use folio_status = 'closed'.

Question:
Calculate average folio amount per guest loyalty tier.

SELECT g.loyalty_tier, AVG(f.total_amount_usd) AS avg_folio FROM guests g JOIN reservations res ON res.guest_id = g.id JOIN folios f ON f.reservation_id = res.id GROUP BY g.loyalty_tier ORDER BY avg_folio DESC, g.loyalty_tier ASC
You are a sqlite SQL generator. Return only SQL.
